# Daksh Soodini

> Student researcher at Oakridge International School, Hyderabad, India, working on smart irrigation for small and marginal farmers.

## Who
- Name: Daksh Soodini (GitHub: DakshSoodini)
- Location: Hyderabad, Telangana, India
- School: Oakridge International School (CBSE, Grades 11-12)
- Contact: dakshsoodini2009@gmail.com · https://github.com/DakshSoodini

## Work
- PANI (IQL Research): independent research applying Implicit Q-Learning, an offline reinforcement-learning method, to irrigation scheduling for small farms in Telangana. Uses ETc = ET0 x Kc (FAO-56) agronomy, capacitive soil-moisture sensing, and a domain-randomised soil-water simulator; compared against CQL, H2O, and ETc-threshold baselines via a comparative greenhouse study.
- Provisional Patent (filed June 2026): "Soil Tension-Activated Hydraulic Emitter with spring tunable crop specific threshold for demand driven irrigation". A fully passive emitter with no electronics or external power: a buried porous ceramic capsule (0.5-1.0 micron pore, 40-60 kPa air entry) senses soil matric potential, which deflects a silicone diaphragm and opens a spring-loaded valve. An interchangeable compression spring (0.3-0.5 N/mm) sets a crop-specific opening threshold. Being trialled in a polyhouse on mulched raised beds with cucumber.
- Project Paani: teaching initiative with the Vivekananda Institute of Human Excellence, Hyderabad, teaching underprivileged children how need-based irrigation works. Launching August 2026.
- Other projects: a crop-price prediction website (Python/ML) and a sign-language-to-speech computer-vision prototype (Negotium finalist).
- Volunteering: Council for Green Revolution (CGR India), an NSE Social Stock Exchange-listed environmental NGO: contributed web development. The PANI research is independent of CGR.

## Pages
- Portfolio (all sections on one page): https://dakshsoodini.github.io/
